title: The Camp
summary: The home base overview, its stations, and why players return to it.
intro: The camp is a useful example of a systems-heavy wiki page because it combines narrative, upgrades, and player services in one space.
facts: This page can later branch into subpages for crafting, companions, upgrades, and camp events.
A strong camp page usually explains both the fiction of the hub and the practical reason players care about it.
The camp URL slug is the-camp.

sections.heading: Purpose
paragraphs: The camp functions as the emotional and mechanical reset point between expeditions. It gives the player a place to plan, recover, and see the consequences of recent events.
items: Safe space for dialogue and downtime scenes.
Central location for upgrades and progression tracking.
Natural place to recap active goals before the next outing.

sections.heading: Stations
paragraphs: Breaking the camp into stations gives the wiki a predictable structure. Each system can stay readable here before it eventually earns a page of its own.
items: Workshop for gear tuning, crafting, or repair.
Planning table for map review and mission selection.
Firepit or commons area for conversation scenes and morale beats.

sections.heading: Growth Over Time
paragraphs: A camp becomes more memorable when it visibly changes. Visual upgrades, new interactions, and fresh ambient dialogue make the hub feel connected to player progress.
items: Add visual changes after story milestones.
Unlock companion interactions through trust or quest completion.
Let new tools at camp feed directly into field exploration.
